original_hypothesis.title: Registration gating can cause unexpected permanent NFT lock if AccountManager registration is revoked/changed after staking
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: All user entry points (stake/unstake/transfer/farm) call _getMainAccountRequireRegistered which reverts if Player.registrationDate == 0. If AccountManager has any mechanism that can reset registrationDate to 0 (e.g., migration, slashing, admin correction, bug), a previously staked user may become unable to call farm/unstake/transfer, leaving NFTs custodied indefinitely.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a cross-contract operational dependency risk: LandManager assumes registration is immutable. If that assumption fails, users can be bricked without any direct LandManager bug.
deployment_assumptions: AccountManager can change a user's registrationDate from non-zero to zero after staking (via admin, migration, or bug)
No alternative admin rescue exists for NFTs
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LandManager.sol:_getMainAccountRequireRegistered() hard-reverts when registrationDate==0; no local escape hatch. AccountManager implementation not provided here.
preconditions: User has staked NFTs (LandManager is current owner of tokenId)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: After migrations or admin maintenance, previously active users find all LandManager actions revert with PlayerNotRegisteredError
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Depends on AccountManager behavior and any privileged operations there
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Persistent until registration restored or NFTs rescued

operational_questions: Is registrationDate immutable once set in AccountManager?
Are there migration/upgrade paths that reset or fail to migrate registration state?
Is there a documented incident-response plan for recovering custodied NFTs in this case?
validation_requirements: Review AccountManager for any function that can clear registration or change mainAccount mapping
Test fork scenario where registrationDate is cleared post-stake and confirm inability to unstake
Check for emergency withdrawal hooks across the system

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: A system change clears or fails to migrate user registration
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: User cannot call any LandManager function, including unstake, due to hard revert
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: NFT remains locked until out-of-band remediation
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires AccountManager state change; not purely attacker-driven

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Localized but severe: affected users lose access to withdraw NFTs
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All LandManager user operations for affected accounts

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Spike in PlayerNotRegisteredError reverts; correlation with AccountManager upgrades
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Large number of NFTs custodied with inactive owners
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Impacted accounts cluster around migrated cohorts

validation_result.mechanism: registration-gated NFT custody with no escape hatch
validation_result.impact_summary: If AccountManager's registrationDate for a user becomes 0 after staking, all LandManager functions (including unstakeMunchable) revert, permanently locking the user's NFTs in the contract with no recovery path.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The LandManager contract custodies ERC-721 NFTs via stakeMunchable (transferFrom to the contract). The ONLY path to retrieve those NFTs is unstakeMunchable. However, unstakeMunchable is double-gated by _getMainAccountRequireRegistered: once through the forceFarmPlots modifier (which calls _farmPlots, which calls _getMainAccountRequireRegistered), and once directly in the function body. _getMainAccountRequireRegistered hard-reverts with PlayerNotRegisteredError when registrationDate == 0. There is NO emergency withdrawal, admin rescue, or alternative withdrawal mechanism anywhere in the contract. If the external AccountManager contract's state changes such that registrationDate becomes 0 (via admin action, migration, upgrade, or bug), the user's NFTs become permanently locked. This is a code defect in LandManager: custodied assets should always have a guaranteed recovery path, and the registration check is unnecessary for the core operation of returning an NFT to its verified owner (munchableOwner[tokenId]).
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines 317-332 show _getMainAccountRequireRegistered hard-reverting on registrationDate == 0. Lines 195-217 show unstakeMunchable using both the forceFarmPlots modifier (line 197) and a direct call to _getMainAccountRequireRegistered (line 199). The forceFarmPlots modifier at line 42 calls _farmPlots, which at line 247 calls _getMainAccountRequireRegistered. No emergency withdrawal function exists in the entire contract. The contract takes NFT custody at line 170 (munchNFT.transferFrom) and returns it only at line 214 (munchNFT.transferFrom inside unstakeMunchable). There is no other code path that can transfer custodied NFTs out.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Hard reverts when registrationDate == 0, blocking all LandManager operations including NFT withdrawal. If AccountManager state changes post-staking, users lose access to custodied NFTs.

The only NFT withdrawal path is double-gated by registration: (1) forceFarmPlots modifier calls _farmPlots which calls _getMainAccountRequireRegistered, and (2) the function body directly calls _getMainAccountRequireRegistered. Both must pass for withdrawal.
This modifier calls _farmPlots before the function body executes. _farmPlots calls _getMainAccountRequireRegistered, so even if the unstake body were safe, this modifier blocks unregistered users.

calling_sequence: 7. Transaction reverts — NFT remains locked in contract
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The entire withdrawal path is: unstakeMunchable → forceFarmPlots modifier → _farmPlots → _getMainAccountRequireRegistered → accountManager.getPlayer → registrationDate check → revert. Even if _farmPlots were somehow bypassed, the direct call to _getMainAccountRequireRegistered at line 199 in unstakeMunchable's body would also revert. There is no admin function, emergency withdrawal, or alternative path to retrieve custodied NFTs. The contract stores the original owner in munchableOwner[tokenId] and has the NFT in custody, but provides absolutely no way to return it if registration is lost.

deployment_assumptions_checked: AccountManager can change a user's registrationDate from non-zero to zero after staking
No alternative admin rescue exists for NFTs
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts (upgrade-proxy-contract.ts) show LandManager is deployed behind a proxy and can be upgraded, but no emergency withdrawal function is added. The AccountManager implementation is not provided in any script or source. Scripts confirm AccountManager is a separate upgradeable proxy contract (accountManagerProxy referenced in deployment cache). The upgrade script shows both accountManager and landManager can be independently upgraded, meaning AccountManager behavior could change via upgrade without LandManager awareness.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario is: (1) User stakes NFT via stakeMunchable — NFT transferred to LandManager contract. (2) Some time later, AccountManager's getPlayer returns registrationDate == 0 for this user (via admin action, migration, upgrade, or bug in AccountManager). (3) User attempts unstakeMunchable — forceFarmPlots modifier calls _farmPlots which calls _getMainAccountRequireRegistered, which reverts with PlayerNotRegisteredError. (4) User cannot call any LandManager function: farmPlots, unstakeMunchable, transferToUnoccupiedPlot all gate on registration. (5) NFT remains permanently locked in the contract. The attack is not purely user-driven but could occur during system migrations/upgrades, admin operations on AccountManager, or bugs. The code defect in LandManager is the absence of any fallback withdrawal mechanism.
validation_result.impact_assessment: If the precondition is met (registrationDate becomes 0 for a staked user), the impact is permanent loss of NFTs. All staked NFTs for the affected account become irrecoverable through normal contract interactions. The only theoretical recovery would be a contract upgrade adding an emergency withdrawal function, which requires admin coordination. Multiple users could be affected simultaneously if a migration or upgrade resets registration state for a cohort of accounts.
recommended_mitigations: Add an emergency withdrawal function that allows the verified owner (munchableOwner[tokenId]) to reclaim their NFT regardless of registration status, possibly with a timelock or admin approval
Remove the registration check from unstakeMunchable or use a softer check that allows withdrawal even if unregistered (the munchableOwner mapping already tracks the rightful owner)
Separate the farming logic from the unstaking path — do not force-farm during unstake, or handle farming failures gracefully (try-catch) so unstake can still succeed
Add a pause-aware emergency withdrawal that works even when the contract is paused
Consider making the forceFarmPlots modifier on unstake skip farming if registration is invalid, rather than reverting the entire transaction
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LandManager and mock AccountManager where registrationDate can be set. 2. Register user, stake NFT. 3. Reset user's registrationDate to 0 in mock AccountManager. 4. Attempt unstakeMunchable — confirm revert with PlayerNotRegisteredError. 5. Attempt all other functions — confirm all revert. 6. Verify no other code path can extract the NFT.
uncertainties: AccountManager implementation is not provided — cannot verify if registrationDate can actually be reset to 0 after initial registration
The proxy upgrade mechanism could theoretically be used to add an emergency withdrawal function, but this requires admin action and is not part of the current code
It's possible that AccountManager has invariants that prevent registrationDate from being cleared, but this cannot be verified from provided code
additional_testing_needed: Review full AccountManager implementation for any function that can clear/reset registrationDate
Check if AccountManager upgrade scripts ever reset or fail to migrate player registration state
Test the full lifecycle: stake → upgrade AccountManager → attempt unstake
Verify if any admin/governance process exists for recovering locked NFTs outside the contract
